intro: Achyronia incurva, also known as the curved senna or sickle bush, is a perennial shrub native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Achyronia incurva include glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
